ROOT CAUSE (deterministically reproduced, CollectionRegistryTest): catalog_collections lock convoy — every batch write ran INSERT..ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING on the SAME (tenant,name) row; PG blocks that statement on ANY uncommitted concurrent write to the conflicting row; blocked txns hoard HikariCP connections -> pool exhaustion -> SQLTransientConnectionException -> opaque 500 (matches the ~16/21 shakeout failure rate). FIX: (1) process-local CollectionRegistry known-pairs cache, marked ONLY post-commit (verified at all 6 call sites — never inside the withTenant lambda, rollback cannot poison the cache); (2) PgVectorRepository.upsertChunksInternal batched per-chunk INSERT loop into one multi-row statement (another un-batching instance, shrinks the blocking window); (3) SQLTransientConnectionException -> typed 503 in Chash/Vector handlers (retryable, pairs with the client ladder). Honest test limitation documented: localhost Testcontainers can't discriminate pre/post at the HTTP level; the deterministic lock-wait test is the mechanism proof. REACHES CLOUD ONLY via the next engine cut — live 500s persist until v0.1.20 deploys.
